name: South Sudan

colors.symbolism: the blood shed in the long struggle for freedom
the land, agriculture, and natural resources
peace, achieved after many years of conflict (the fimbriations between the main stripes)
the waters of the Nile, the source of life (the hoist triangle)
unity, determination, and the bright future of the nation (the star within the triangle)

description: Three broad horizontal stripes — black, red, green — separated by thin white fimbriations, with a blue isoceles triangle at the hoist bearing a centred yellow five-pointed star.
history: Adopted on 9 July 2005 as the flag of the autonomous Government of Southern Sudan under the Comprehensive Peace Agreement, and retained as the national flag at independence on 9 July 2011. The design is based on the flag of the Sudan People's Liberation Movement/Army (SPLM/A), which led the long civil war against the Khartoum government. The Star of Bethlehem within the blue triangle is identified by name in the Transitional Constitution.

features.symbolism: Pan-African colours separated by thin white fimbriations: black for the people of South Sudan, red for the blood shed for freedom, green for the country's land, with white representing peace
the waters of the Nile and the source of life
unity of the states of South Sudan and the nation's determination to lead the country to a bright future
